info.title: Global API Beta
info.description:
Bandwidth's global API is an application programming interface for Bandwidth's global network.

## Versions

|API Version|Description|Documentation|
|:---:|:---:|:---|
|`v1`|Our current API, which includes number ordering, compliance, configuration and inventory management to support your Voice and SMS communications.| [Click here](https://dev.bandwidth.com/apis/legacy-apis/global/)|
|`v2`|Our CDRs (Call Detail Records) API.|[Click here](https://dev.bandwidth.com/apis/legacy-apis/global/v2/)|
|`v3`|Our Porting API in Limited Availability|[Click here](https://dev.bandwidth.com/apis/legacy-apis/global/v3/)|
|`beta`|Early access to APIs under development including Number Whitelisting.|Currently viewing|

## Global Emergency Address and Endpoint Provisioning

Please refer to the [Unified Emergency Services Provisioning API](https://dev.bandwidth.com/apis/emergency-provisioning/) guide to automate the provisioning process for endpoints and emergency addresses on the Global Portal.

## Access

Authentication for Bandwidth's global API is done with API keys. Please ensure:

- Your account is activated for API access. You can do so on our [Global Portal](https://app.voxbone.com/home). If you have trouble, reach out to your account manager or raise a support ticket.
- You've setup an API key. You can generate your API key under **Settings > API**.

## Environments

There are currently two environments for our global APIs:

| Environment | Base URL | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Production | https://api.voxbone.com | Live production network. |
| Sandbox | https://api.sandbox.voxbone.com | Intended for simulating ordering and address verification. You can test ordering without being charged, service activation and more. Any API calls performed through our Sandbox API can be viewed via our [Sandbox Portal](https://sandbox.voxbone.com/sandbox-v3/). |

## Security & Rate Limiting

For protecting both your apps and our systems, a __20 requests per second by client IP__ is performed on your API calls. If you send more requests, your requests may be rejected by an `HTTP 509 Bandwidth Limit Exceeded` or `HTTP 429 Too Many Requests` in the response.

tags.name: Number Whitelisting
tags.description: Number Whitelisting let's you take advantage of Bandwidth's network, but without necessarily buying Bandwidth numbers.

get.description: Allows you to retrieve your whitelisted inventory. Filters and sorting parameters are supported.

put.description: Allows you to add a number to your whitelist inventory. In case a number is already present in your inventory, it will be updated.

get.description: Allows you to retrieve all info about a specific number in your whitelisted inventory.

delete.description: Allows you to remove a number from your whitelisted inventory.

parameters.name: e164
parameters.description: The DID in its E164 format (e.g. +16469185038) with the ability to provide multiple E164s.

/whitelist/{e164}.x-summary: List or delete a specific whitelisted number

status.description: Status of the HTTP response from the API.

countryCodeA3.description: Indicates the country code of the DID in its ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 format (e.g. 'GBR' for United Kingdom, 'USA' for United States of America, 'BEL' for Belgium, etc.).

e164.description: The DID in its E164 format (e.g. +16469185038) with the ability to provide multiple E164s.

zone.description: Zone is the trunk that you would like to use with the number you are activating. E.g. WORLD, A, B, C, GBR, CAN, USA, etc... If you use Bandwidth on a PAYG model, you should not add the Zone field in your query.

status.description: Status of the HTTP response from the API.

limit.description: A limit on the number of records to be returned.

offset.description: Indicates the number of records that have been skipped before the first record is selected.

Pagination.description: An object which contains pagination information related to the response.

apiKey.description: Authentication to Bandwidth's global API is handled via key based authentication. You can manage your API key on our [Global Portal](https://www.bandwidth.com/login/) under **Settings > API**.
apiKey.name: apikey
